channel.title: Eloquent on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions
channel.description: Recent content in Eloquent on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions

item.title: [Solution] Laravel Model Not Found Fix
item.description:
Laravel Model Not Found

Fix Eloquent ModelNotFoundException when a model record is not found. Handle missing records, route model binding, and query issues..

What This Error Means

Common error scenarios include:

  • Connection or network failures
  • Invalid configuration or options
  • Resource not found or unavailable
  • Permission or access denied

Common Causes

<?php
// Cause 1: Incorrect configuration or missing setup
// Cause 2: Network or connection issues
// Cause 3: Invalid input or parameters
// Cause 4: Missing dependencies or resources
?>

How to Fix

Fix 1: Verify configuration and setup

<?php
// Check configuration values and ensure required setup
// Verify the package/library is properly configured
?>

Fix 2: Add proper error handling

<?php
try {
 // Use the package/library with proper error handling
} catch (\Exception $e) {
 error_log('Error: ' . $e->getMessage());
 // Handle gracefully
}
?>

Fix 3: Validate input and add checks

<?php
// Validate input before processing
// Check existence before accessing resources
// Use type declarations and strict comparisons
?>

Examples

<?php
// Common error handling pattern
try {
 $result = doSomething();
 echo $result;
} catch (\Exception $e) {
 error_log('Error: ' . $e->getMessage());
 http_response_code(500);
 echo json_encode(['error' => $e->getMessage()]);
}
?>

Related Errors

  • PHP Fatal Error — fatal error
  • PHP Warning — warning
  • PHP Notice — notice

item.title: Database migration failed
item.description:
This error occurs when a Laravel database migration fails during php artisan migrate. The underlying database rejects the migration SQL due to schema conflicts, missing dependencies, or constraint violations.

Common Causes

  • Column or table already exists when creating it
  • Foreign key references a table that has not been created yet
  • Column type mismatch with existing data
  • Missing database permissions to alter tables
  • Migration file contains invalid SQL syntax

How to Fix

  1. Check migration status before retrying:
php artisan migrate:status
  1. Use Schema::hasTable() and Schema::hasColumn() to avoid duplicate creation:
Schema::create('users', function (Blueprint $table) {
 if (!Schema::hasTable('users')) {
 $table->id();
 $table->string('email')->unique();
 $table->timestamps();
 }
});
  1. Ensure dependent tables are created first by ordering migrations:
Schema::create('posts', function (Blueprint $table) {
 $table->id();
 $table->foreignId('user_id')->constrained();
 $table->string('title');
 $table->timestamps();
});
  1. Wrap risky migrations in try-catch for better error handling:
use Illuminate\Database\QueryException;

try {
 Schema::table('orders', function (Blueprint $table) {
 $table->foreign('payment_id')->references('id')->on('payments');
 });
} catch (QueryException $e) {
 Log::error('Migration failed: ' . $e->getMessage());
}
  1. Rollback and retry after fixing the migration:
php artisan migrate:rollback
php artisan migrate

Examples

// Migration referencing a table that doesn't exist yet
Schema::table('orders', function (Blueprint $table) {
 $table->foreignId('payment_id')->constrained();
 // QueryException if 'payments' table doesn't exist
});

// Adding a column that already exists
Schema::table('users', function (Blueprint $table) {
 $table->string('email')->unique();
 // QueryException if 'email' column already exists
});

Related Errors

  • Model not found
  • Service container resolution error

item.title: Eloquent query error
item.description:
This error occurs when an Eloquent query or Query Builder method fails, typically due to SQL syntax errors, column not found, or constraint violations.

Common Causes

  • Query references a column that does not exist in the database
  • Unique or foreign key constraint violation
  • Invalid SQL syntax in raw queries
  • Model not migrated (table does not exist)

How to Fix

  1. Check the SQL being generated with toSql():
$query = User::where('email', 'test@example.com');
echo $query->toSql(); // Debug the query
echo $query->count(); // Execute
  1. Use try-catch to handle query exceptions:
use Illuminate\Database\QueryException;

try {
 User::create(['email' => 'test@example.com', 'name' => 'Alice']);
} catch (QueryException $e) {
 if ($e->getCode() === '23000') {
 return response()->json(['error' => 'Email already exists'], 409);
 }
 throw $e;
}
  1. Use DB::beginTransaction() for complex operations:
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\DB;

DB::beginTransaction();
try {
 Account::where('id', 1)->decrement('balance', 100);
 Account::where('id', 2)->increment('balance', 100);
 DB::commit();
} catch (\Exception $e) {
 DB::rollBack();
 throw $e;
}

Examples

// Column does not exist
User::where('emal', 'test@example.com')->first();
// QueryException: SQLSTATE[42S22]: Column not found: 1054 Unknown column 'emal'

Related Errors

  • Method not found on instance

item.title: Method X does not exist on instance
item.description:
This error occurs when you call a method on an object that does not have that method defined. In Laravel projects it frequently happens with Eloquent models when accessing relationships via method syntax without defining them, or when using a Facade that resolves to an unexpected class.

Common Causes

  • Calling a relationship method (e.g. $user->posts()) without defining it on the model
  • Using an outdated package version where a method was removed or renamed
  • Calling a method on a Facade that resolves to a different underlying class than expected
  • Typo in the method name

How to Fix

Define the relationship method on the Eloquent model:

item.title: Migration failed - QueryException
item.description:
This error occurs when a Laravel database migration fails during php artisan migrate. It is thrown as Illuminate\Database\QueryException when the underlying SQL statement cannot be executed.

Common Causes

  • Migration references a column or table that already exists
  • Foreign key references a table that has not been created yet
  • Column type mismatch with existing data
  • Missing database permissions to alter tables
  • Migration file contains invalid SQL syntax

How to Fix

  1. Check the SQL being generated by the migration:
Schema::create('users', function (Blueprint $table) {
 $table->id();
 $table->string('email')->unique();
 $table->timestamps();
});

// Debug the query
$blueprint->build(new MySqlGrammar, new Connection);
  1. Wrap migrations in try-catch for better error handling:
use Illuminate\Database\QueryException;

try {
 Schema::table('users', function (Blueprint $table) {
 $table->foreign('team_id')->references('id')->on('teams');
 });
} catch (QueryException $e) {
 Log::error('Migration failed: ' . $e->getMessage());
 // Handle the error appropriately
}
  1. Use migrate:status to check which migrations have run:
php artisan migrate:status
  1. Rollback and retry if needed:
php artisan migrate:rollback
php artisan migrate

Examples

// Migration that references a table that doesn't exist yet
Schema::table('orders', function (Blueprint $table) {
 $table->foreignId('payment_id')->constrained();
 // QueryException if 'payments' table doesn't exist
});

Related Errors

  • Eloquent query error
  • Validation error

item.title: ModelNotFoundException - model not found
item.description:
This error occurs when you call findOrFail() or firstOrFail() on an Eloquent model and no matching record exists. Laravel throws Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\ModelNotFoundException.

Common Causes

  • Record was deleted but application still references its ID
  • Incorrect ID passed to findOrFail() or route model binding
  • Query conditions filter out all matching records
  • Database was reset or migrated without seeding

How to Fix

  1. Use find() instead of findOrFail() when the record may not exist:
$user = User::find($id);

if (!$user) {
 return response()->json(['error' => 'User not found'], 404);
}
  1. Register a custom ModelNotFoundException handler:
use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\ModelNotFoundException;

public function register()
{
 $this->renderable(function (ModelNotFoundException $e, $request) {
 return response()->json([
 'error' => 'Resource not found',
 'message' => class_basename($e->getModel()) . ' not found',
 ], 404);
 });
}
  1. Use route model binding with a fallback route:
Route::get('/users/{user?}', [UserController::class, 'show']);

public function show(?User $user)
{
 if (!$user) {
 return response()->json(['error' => 'User not found'], 404);
 }
}

Examples

// Throws ModelNotFoundException if no user with id=999
$user = User::findOrFail(999);

// Using route model binding — throws if slug doesn't match
Route::get('/posts/{post:slug}', [PostController::class, 'show']);

// Safe lookup with default
$user = User::findOr($id, fn () => User::factory()->make());

Related Errors

  • Route not found
  • File not found

item.title: ModelNotFoundException - model not found
item.description:
This error occurs when you call findOrFail() or firstOrFail() on an Eloquent model and no matching record exists in the database. Laravel throws Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\ModelNotFoundException.

Common Causes

  • Record was deleted but application still references its ID
  • Incorrect ID passed to findOrFail() or route model binding
  • Query conditions filter out all matching records
  • Database was reset or migrated without seeding

How to Fix

  1. Use find() instead of findOrFail() when the record may not exist:
$user = User::find($id);

if (!$user) {
 return response()->json(['error' => 'User not found'], 404);
}
  1. Register a custom ModelNotFoundException handler in Handler.php:
public function register()
{
 $this->renderable(function (ModelNotFoundException $e, $request) {
 return response()->json([
 'error' => 'Resource not found',
 'message' => $e->getModel() . ' not found',
 ], 404);
 });
}
  1. Use route model binding with a custom key:
Route::get('/users/{user:slug}', [UserController::class, 'show']);

Examples

// This throws ModelNotFoundException if no user with id=999
$user = User::findOrFail(999);

// Using route model binding — throws if slug doesn't match
Route::get('/posts/{post:slug}', [PostController::class, 'show']);

Related Errors

  • Route not found
  • Eloquent query error
